methods.description: Process for analyzing pollen in sediment samples to confirm the correlation of sediment cores and ensure reliable correlation of study results.

steps.description: A sediment volume of 1e3 cm³ was used per sample. Lycopodium tablets were added before chemical treatment for estimation of pollen concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment samples with Lycopodium tablets for pollen concentration estimation

steps.description: Sample processing followed Eisele et al. (1994) and comprised treatment with 10% HCl, 10% NaOH and 40-45% HF, followed by acetolysis.
steps.searchable_summary: chemically treat samples with HCl, NaOH, HF, and perform acetolysis

steps.description: Samples with high concentrations of clastic material were subjected to density separation using sodium polytungstate.
steps.searchable_summary: perform density separation on samples with sodium polytungstate

steps.description: Residues were embedded in 'Kaiser's glycerine jelly', fixed on microscope slides and analysed with a Carl Zeiss Axio Scope A1® microscope (400-1000x magnification).
steps.searchable_summary: embed residues in glycerine jelly and analyze with microscope

methods.method_name: X-ray Fluorescence Core Scanning and LOI
methods.description: Method for scanning sediment cores using X-ray fluorescence to correlate sediment cores and measure organic matter content.

steps.description: X-ray fluorescence core scanning was performed on all cores using an Avaatech (GEN-4) XRF core scanner.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRF core scanning on sediment cores

steps.description: A 10 kV Rh anode X-ray tube without a filter was used with a spatial resolution of 5 mm, a counting time of 10 s, a 150 mA current, and a slit size of 10 mm crosscore and 5 mm downcore.
steps.searchable_summary: configure XRF scanner with specific settings for scanning

steps.description: A 4-mm-thick Ultralene® foil was placed over the core halves after they were smoothed for analysis to avoid core desiccation and contamination of the detector window.
steps.searchable_summary: cover core halves with Ultralene foil for protection

steps.description: The bAxilBatch software was used to process the X-ray spectra.
steps.searchable_summary: process X-ray spectra using bAxilBatch software

steps.description: Sedimentary organic matter content measurements were taken every 4 cm in both the FURA and FU1/3 cores using LOI at 550°C.
steps.searchable_summary: measure organic matter content using LOI method

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing chironomid samples from sediment cores to produce temperature reconstructions.

steps.description: Forty-six samples were selected for chironomid analysis from the FURA cores and 71 samples from the FU1/FU3 cores.
steps.searchable_summary: select samples for chironomid analysis from cores

steps.description: Samples above 9.6 m required no chemical pre-treatment. Samples below 9.60 m were heated in 10% KOH for 15 min at 85°C.
steps.searchable_summary: chemically treat samples below 9.60 m with KOH

steps.description: Samples were sieved with 100 mm mesh-size, and chironomid head capsules were picked from a Bogorov tray under a stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples and pick chironomid head capsules

steps.description: Samples were then dried and mounted in Euparal before identification at 40-100x magnification using a compound microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and mount samples in Euparal for identification

methods.description: Numerical analysis to determine zonations in the chironomid record and summarize major changes in the chironomid assemblage.

steps.description: The clustering algorithm CONISS was used to determine zonations in the chironomid record.
steps.searchable_summary: apply CONISS algorithm for zonation determination

steps.description: Zonations were tested for statistical significance using a Broken Stick Model.
steps.searchable_summary: test zonations for significance using Broken Stick Model

steps.description: R studio was used to calculate CONISS using the rioja package.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate CONISS using rioja package in R

steps.description: A Detrended Correspondence Analysis (DCA) was used to summarize major changes in the chironomid assemblage using CANOCO 5.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DCA using CANOCO 5 for chironomid assemblage
